implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-007 deterministic entity_id for split-brain dedupe
AC-008 lint/format hygiene (88-char, snake_case, type hints, docstrings)
requirements_remaining: BDD step impls for the listed scenarios — explicitly out of scope per task spec ('TASK-IC-011').

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pre-write existence check by build_id in forge_pipeline_history
completion_promises.evidence: write_session_outcome calls the injected SessionOutcomeExistsCheck (async callable typed Callable[[str], Awaitable[bool]]) with the build_id BEFORE collecting decisions or constructing the entity. Verified by TestPreWriteExistenceCheck::test_existence_check_called_with_build_id and the parameter contract documented in the module docstring 'Pre-write existence check'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Existing entity → no-op return None (@edge-case session-outcome-retry-idempotency)
completion_promises.evidence: When exists_check returns True, write_session_outcome returns None without invoking the SQLite repo or the writer. Tests TestPreWriteExistenceCheck::test_existing_outcome_returns_none_no_op, test_existing_outcome_does_not_write, and test_existing_outcome_does_not_query_repo verify each branch of the no-op path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: GateDecision references collected from SQLite repo (read-only via FEAT-FORGE-001 interface)
completion_promises.evidence: PipelineHistoryRepository is declared as a typing.Protocol with a single read method get_gate_decisions_for_build(build_id) -> Sequence[GateDecision]. The module imports nothing from FEAT-FORGE-001 and has no write surface against SQLite. TestGateDecisionCollection::test_repo_queried_with_build_id confirms the repository is queried with the build_id; test_repo_without_required_method_raises_typeerror enforces the Protocol shape at runtime.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Sort by decided_at ASC before constructing gate_decision_ids list (ASSUM-008 resolution)
completion_promises.evidence: _sort_gate_decisions returns sorted(decisions, key=lambda d: (d.decided_at, str(d.entity_id))) — primary by decided_at ASC, secondary by entity_id for deterministic tie-breaking when timestamps collide at microsecond resolution. Verified by TestOrderingByDecidedAt::test_out_of_order_decisions_are_sorted_on_output and test_microsecond_tied_timestamps_use_entity_id_tiebreaker; test_pure_helper_does_not_mutate_input pins the immutability contract.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Writes via write_entity() (synchronous, NOT fire-and-forget — caller wants confirmation)
completion_promises.evidence: The default 'write' parameter is forge.memory.writer.write_entity, awaited directly inside write_session_outcome (no asyncio.ensure_future / no thread). Failures from write_entity propagate to the caller. TestSynchronousWrite::test_write_invoked_with_session_outcome_and_group_id verifies the call shape (PIPELINE_HISTORY_GROUP_ID + built SessionOutcome). test_write_failure_propagates_to_caller verifies the synchronous-failure contract.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Only fires on first terminal-state transition; in-progress states produce no SessionOutcome (@edge-case no-in-progress-session-outcome)
completion_promises.evidence: _is_terminal(outcome) is checked before the existence check; non-terminal outcomes (in_progress, running, pending, queued, '', SUCCESS) short-circuit to a logged warning + None return. TestTerminalOnly::test_non_terminal_outcome_returns_none parametrises over six non-terminal strings; test_non_terminal_does_not_query_existence proves the short-circuit happens before any I/O; test_non_terminal_logs_warning_with_build_id verifies the structured log line.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Concurrent calls (two Forge instances) → both see the same existence check; one writes, one no-ops (@concurrency gate-decisions-in-close-succession and split-brain safety from TASK-IC-001 entity_id contract)
completion_promises.evidence: _session_outcome_entity_id derives a deterministic UUID5 from build_id under a namespaced URI prefix, so two writers for the same build produce the same entity_id (Graphiti upsert-key dedupe — TASK-IC-001 contract). TestConcurrentDedupeContract: test_entity_id_is_deterministic_for_build_id, test_entity_id_differs_for_distinct_build_ids, test_concurrent_callers_one_writes_one_no_ops (stateful exists-check simulating two-instance race), and test_split_brain_writes_share_entity_id (worst-case both-see-False race verifies the entity_id remains identical).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: No ruff/black/mypy/flake8 configuration is present in pyproject.toml. Source and test files were authored to satisfy the .claude/rules/code-style.md guide: snake_case, PascalCase, type hints, max 88-char lines (verified by awk 'length > 88' returning zero matches on both files), f-strings, context managers, list comprehensions, no relative-style imports outside the package.
